purpose: MLX continued-pretraining smoke on Qwen3-0.6B-Base, 500 iters
data: runs/local-integrity-2026-05-08/H_cpt_data (5000 train + 500 valid, prompt='' + completion plain text)

stability_gap_analysis.interpretation: Initial 100-step transient spike likely reflects LoRA adapter warmup on cold model + lr=2e-4 too aggressive for the very small effective batch (bs=1). After the spike the val loss settles into a 5.4-5.9 nat band with mild oscillation. Smoke verifies: (a) MLX CPT path runs end-to-end, (b) data format accepted, (c) checkpoints save. NOT a robust convergence demo: 500 iters at bs=1 is too short for cold CPT on 5K corpus. Production path on RunPod uses chain_train_round2.sh CPT stage (full Phase-3 settings). arXiv:2406.14833 cites stability instability for naive CPT — confirmed observable here, mitigation handled in production via warmup + lr_scheduler.
stability_gap_analysis.production_mitigation_path: train_cpt.py uses warmup + cosine schedule per Phase-3 wiring (see PR #5 R2 follow-up)

verdict_notes: Path verified: MLX accepts prompt='' + completion plain-text format for CPT-style training.
Initial-spike instability (1.53 nat) detected but recovered; production path applies warmup + scheduler.
